name: Jonah Jones
abstract: Jonah Jones (born Robert Elliott Jones; December 31, 1909 – April 30, 2000) was a jazz trumpeter who created concise versions of jazz and swing and jazz standards that appealed to a mass audience. In the jazz community, he is known for his work with Stuff Smith.

about_quotes.speaker: Jonah Jones
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee explains that 'Jonah' is a nickname derived from his own last name 'Jones' and a mistake he made while playing in a band. He is not referring to a specific person named Jonah Jones, but rather to himself.

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Barefield
about_quotes.evidence: Jonah Jones and Doc Cheatham and Shad Collins were the trumpets... And we went down to Uruguay to play for the festival down there

about_quotes.speaker: Hinton Milt
about_quotes.evidence: we got the seven piece band, then it went down to four pieces, just Jonah Jones, David Rivera, Panama Francis and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hinton Milt — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Johnny Varro
about_quotes.evidence: I was on a band once where I was the only white kid in the band. There was Jonah Jones on trumpet... and myself
about_quotes.doc_title: Johnny Varro interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 16, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Maxwell
about_quotes.evidence: In fact, Jonah took some lessons from me... And then everytime he'd be in town he'd come for some more lessons.

about_quotes.speaker: Horace Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: he joined me when I was going to make a tour... and we went to Buffalo, New York, and played quite a few dates
